description_long: This object is a weathered, rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit, commonly seen attached to the exterior of urban buildings. The main body is constructed from metal panels with visible rust and discoloration, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements. One side features a large circular fan protected by a wire mesh grille, while the opposite side has a series of horizontal metal bars and vent slits for airflow. The ends of the unit display additional vent openings and two protruding access panels, likely for electrical or refrigerant connections. The overall appearance is industrial and utilitarian, with mounting feet at the base for secure installation on a flat surface.
description: A rusted, rectangular outdoor air conditioner with a large fan, wire mesh grille, and multiple vented panels, showing signs of weathering and industrial use.
description_view_0: The back view shows a flat, rusted metal panel with horizontal bars and a few vent slits, lacking any major openings or fans.
description_view_1: The left side reveals a series of vertical vent slits with visible coiled tubing inside, providing airflow and heat exchange.
description_view_2: The front view displays a prominent circular fan behind a wire mesh grille, with subtle panel lines and weathered metal surfaces.
description_view_3: The right side features two rectangular access panels, likely for service or electrical connections, and a mostly solid, weathered surface.
attribute_grille_pattern: The fan grille is a circular wire mesh with a crisscross diamond pattern, while the rear grille consists of horizontal metal bars spaced evenly apart.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: The exhaust vents are located on the left side, featuring multiple vertical slits with visible coiled tubing behind them, and additional horizontal bars on the rear for airflow.
